g.data-narration: Ethereum uses an account-based model instead of Bitcoin's UTXO system, enabling rich state management where every address holds balance and data.
g.text: Account-based model enables complex state and smart contracts

g.data-narration: Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs) are controlled by private keys owned by users. They can initiate transactions, transfer ETH, and deploy contracts.
Contract Accounts (CAs) are controlled by smart contract code. They cannot initiate transactions but execute logic when triggered by EOAs or other contracts.
Every account has four state fields: nonce counts transactions, balance holds Wei, storageRoot holds data hash, and codeHash references executable code.
g.text: EOA: tx count | CA: contracts created
Amount of Wei held (1 ETH = 10^18 Wei)
Merkle hash of contract storage data
Empty string hash for EOAs | contract code hash for CAs

g.data-narration: Only EOAs can sign and submit transactions. Contract accounts can only react to incoming calls, never originate new blockchain activity.
g.text: CRITICAL: All blockchain activity originates from EOA transactions. Contract accounts can only REACT to received calls.
Even internal contract-to-contract messages trace back to an original EOA-signed transaction

g.data-narration: EOA addresses are 20 bytes derived from public key hash. Contract addresses come from creator address plus nonce. Both are 160-bit identifiers.
g.text: EOA: Keccak256(public key) takes last 20 bytes | Contract: Keccak256(creator_address, nonce) takes last 20 bytes

g.data-narration: EOA creation is free and off-chain using cryptographic libraries. Contract creation requires a paid transaction with bytecode sent to null address.
g.text: Free, off-chain. Generate private key → public key → address. Exists on-chain after first funded transaction.
On-chain, costs gas. Send deployment transaction with bytecode to null address (0x0). Creates new account with codeHash.

g.data-narration: Key differences: EOAs have private keys and can initiate transactions; Contracts have immutable code, no private key, and can only respond to calls.
g.text: • EOA: Private key control | Can send transactions | No code | Free creation
• Contract: Code control | Cannot send transactions | Has code | Paid creation
• Both: Have address, balance, nonce, and state in world trie
